iprefer_points::TNRCM.author: Eddie
iprefer_points::TNRCM.content: Dining venue is average compared to the other two hotels, though I had a positive bias from just returning to China. Amenities are quite substantial but not as comfortable as the other two properties, even inferior to American casino hotels. Service is noticeably weaker than the other two, though given my preference not to trouble staff, this doesn't bother me much. The pool, while located underground, has a glass ceiling providing better lighting and water quality than the pools several dozen floors up at the other two hotels. The gym has fewer machines but offers a separate yoga room. The visa application service is genuinely convenient.

iprefer_points::TNRCM.author: yang_gao
iprefer_points::TNRCM.content:
Hotel Name: Tianjin Ritz-Carlton Hotel

Stay Period: May 2021

Booking Channel: FHR Prepay

Booked/Actual Room Type: Booked a basic room, upgraded to a basic suite after check-in

Hotel Membership: Marriott Gold

SNP/Membership Benefits: Received all SNP benefits. Points could be registered to travel companion's account. Travel companion had higher membership status, so points were registered under her account. The 100 hotel credit was split into two parts: 40 for dining and 60 for spa (the split was intentional, likely due to concern about generous usage). Didn't try spa due to high pricing. Platinum members receive 30% discount on dining, which offers good value.

Comments: Although the hotel opened in 2013, it's maintained in excellent condition with strong European aesthetic. The lobby roses smell wonderful. The hotel features numerous artworks, resembling a small museum. Located near Wudadao (Five Roads), very pleasant area. However, Tianjin as a city appears increasingly drained by Beijing, with development seemingly stalled.

iprefer_points::TNRCM.author: Eddie
iprefer_points::TNRCM.content: They upgrade FHR-booked Silver Elite members to suites. Upgraded both stays, very generous. The room looks even better in person than in photos. Breakfast is strictly two servings, cannot use $100 credit for breakfast. One $100 credit was used for self-service dinner with crab theme in October with various crab preparations. Another time had Li Xuan restaurant, slightly higher priced than Yan Ting, no hardcore Cantonese. Appetizer and entree portions were extreme (very small vs. massive), soup was good. But both meals weren't as good as Yan Ting next door. Both times used $100 credit plus extra 150 CNY for three people. Overall service much better than St. Regis next door. Besides welcome fruit, first time received a box of delicious almond cookies, second time received nicely arranged desserts.

iprefer_points::TNRCM.author: herebjldn
iprefer_points::TNRCM.content: Multiple stays, and each time I'm amazed at how excellent the hotel service is. The hotel has been open for a while, so it's natural the decor isn't the most modern, but all facilities are very well maintained. From entering the lobby to checking in and taking the elevator to the room, staff from different departments accompanied us throughout, and every staff member provided timely and thoughtful service with high standards. Breakfast is abundant and delicious, and the Chinese restaurant prices are reasonable. Currently my favorite hotel in Shenzhen, strongly recommend.

iprefer_points::TNRCM.author: garfieldwei
iprefer_points::TNRCM.content: Hotel: The Ritz-Carlton Tysons. Stay: 6/11/2022 - 6/13/2022. Booking: First night FHR prepay, second night free night from official website. Booked room: Double bed (economy). Actual room: Upgraded to king suite, but guest requested change back to double bed room. Membership: Platinum Amex (Titanium status). SNP/benefits: Both nights showed benefits on official website but first night did not receive SNP. First night included $60 breakfast credit + $100 credit; since check-in was late and next day schedule was full, guest negotiated with staff to move the $100 credit to the second night.

unmatched.author: Eddie
unmatched.content: Yuexuan restaurant is the best among these three hotels. The highest-rated dishes are the most affordable options: Buddha's hand with celery and slate-grilled meat. Ordering non-premium items for $100 feels sufficient for three people. Symbolic welcome fruit and pastries were provided, along with FHR welcome letter. The amenities are my favorite among Shanghai's three properties—the rosemary white tea scent is wonderful, and the body lotion is gentle enough to apply directly to the face (the Ritz-Carlton lotion caused slight stinging, while the Shanghai Jing'an version could literally damage your skin). The slippers are the most comfortable with oversized, soft foam soles. Service overall is noticeably better than the Jing'an property. The gym has plenty of equipment but is somewhat crowded.

unmatched.author: Eddie
unmatched.content: LHRC rate includes resort fee; booking deducts the non-resort fee price, and presenting Diamond card at check-in waives the resort fee. Breakfast credit can be applied to dinner, and the Diamond card's $100 credit can be combined for one meal; also settled via Amex to earn Amex offers—effectively free. Dined at an influencer restaurant. Not as good as expected.

unmatched.author: Eddie
unmatched.content: Dinner was quite satisfying. The Continental Breakfast was surprisingly just Starbucks.

unmatched.author: bumblebee
unmatched.content: Delano: all suites, most generous upgrades, largest rooms, excellent surfing pool

unmatched.author: brokenarcher
unmatched.content:
Hotel: Wynn Las Vegas
Stay: June 2021
Booking: FHR
Booked/Actual room: Budget room upgraded to strip view panoramic room, high floor with direct Palazzo view
Hotel membership: None
Benefits received: FHR benefits only - $100 spa credit (converted to food & beverage at check-in), $25 welcome drink (must use on check-in day), $60 breakfast credit ($30 per person), $5 property credit. Paid about $150 total with resort fees but received nearly $200 in credits. All credits exclude tips. Spa-to-F&B conversion is YYMV. Always review your folio at checkout.

unmatched.author: MarriottBonvoy
unmatched.content: Hotel: Mr. C Seaport (New York). Stay: August 2021. Booked via FHR Prepaid. Received a 2-level upgrade from the lowest room type, but this hotel's room types basically only differ by view direction and are all poor. Member benefits included $100 dining credit, though the hotel restaurant is terrible—you'd get better food anywhere else. Breakfast was decent. Warning: avoid this property. Rooms are tiny with sliding bathroom doors. All rooms overlook either the parking lot or opposite rooftops, basically feels like a 4-star hotel in every sense. This seems like a property that got into FHR through connections or payments rather than merit—I hope it gets delisted next year. Only stayed here because after the $200 credit deduction it was cheaper than a Hampton Inn, and as the saying goes, you get what you pay for.

unmatched.author: kaiyoyo
unmatched.content:
Sharing a Shanghai review. When I returned to China a few months ago, this was the only FHR hotel I stayed at; others were booked directly on Hyatt/Marriott websites.

Hotel: Pullman Shanghai Jing'an
Stay: May 2021, 3 days
Booking: FHR Prepay
Room Type: Deluxe King (base category); upgraded by one level but basically indistinguishable since the base room was already spacious with everything.
Membership: None
Benefits: $100 credit for restaurant, bar, spa, or room service

Excellent overall experience. Located in Jing'an Park with beautiful surroundings. À la carte breakfast with above-average quality, better than hotels averaging ¥1500/night. Quiet gym; cold towels were great. Has a Michelin-starred restaurant. Lots of young guests; TAXX2 nightclub is across the street—perfect for heading back to the hotel after clubbing.

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content: Hotel: Beijing Four Seasons. Check-in period: not recalled. Booking channel: Centurion Concierge. Actual room type: Royal Suite. Hotel membership: none.

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content: The food looked good, however...

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content:
Hotel name: W Bangkok
Stay period: Holiday
Booking channel: MasterCard world elite concierge
Booked room type / Actual room type: Fantasy Suite / Top Surprise Suite
Hotel membership: Unknown

unmatched.author: illusionwing
unmatched.content: $100 dining credit used at banquet hall, average-above quality

unmatched.author: illusionwing
unmatched.content: Did not use the credit

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content:
Hotel: Chengdu Lizhi
Stay period: Weekday
Booked via: Centurion Concierge
Actual room: Presidential Suite
Hotel membership: Unknown

Scott-branded hot pot with whirlpool bathtub
Postcard sent while reading in the study
Felt like the whole floor was included, but actually only half was available.

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.hotel_name_en: Unknown - name does not match any recognized hotel
unmatched.content: Hotel: Chengdu (name unclear). Stay period: Weekday. Booking: MasterCard World Elite Concierge. Booked: Ambassador Suite, Received: Presidential Suite. Hotel membership status: Unclear. Toiletries: Hermès.

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content: The presidential suite features a top-floor private pool, though unfortunately a VIP guest was staying there that day.

unmatched.author: anon87878907
unmatched.content: Satisfied. Hotel: Beijing W (Chang'an Street, now delisted). Stay period: Anniversary. Booking: MasterCard World Elite concierge. Booked room type/Actual room type: Budget/Surprise suite. Hotel membership: Unknown. Special note: W brand Maserati transfer service.

unmatched.author: jjzz789
unmatched.content: Hotel: W San Francisco. Stay period: April 2019. Booking channel: FHR. Booked room type/Actual room type: Budget room / High floor with grand view. Hotel membership: None.

unmatched.author: patrickwang
unmatched.content: Should be the newest of the three. The hotel has a fairly business-like feel. Located on the far south end of the Strip - many think it's far, but there's actually a free tram. During check-in, an Asian lady was cold in attitude and didn't give a room upgrade (said it was full but I saw plenty of rooms available on the website). Being a new hotel, the room facilities are still quite comfortable. Same $60 breakfast and $100 dining credit. The $60 breakfast can only be used at the hotel's cafe - very impractical, all pre-made and reheated like Starbucks. Many of the hotel's facilities are shared with Mandalay Bay. The pool is excellent - it has a lazy river and wave machine.

unmatched.author: brokenarcher
unmatched.hotel_name_en: Conrad at Las Vegas Resorts World
unmatched.content: Possibly the first Conrad LVRW review on this forum? It opened at the end of June. I booked it to try a new property and use my Aspire resort credit. Since it was Friday night, the initial cash rate for a basic room was $250+tax. I booked semi-flexible to reimburse the credit. Two weeks before arrival, prices dropped significantly—strip view was around $200, and Expedia had an additional 30% off. I rebooked strip view and after price matching, got it for $100+tax. At check-in, because of long invited guest queues, I was taken to VIP check-in where the staff was very friendly. They offered to upgrade to a 1-bed king suite, but couldn't offer a strip view suite due to full occupancy. Honestly, Resorts World's location is awkward—even a strip view doesn't show much, and there's just a construction site and parking lot in front. The 1-bed suite turned out nice, though the decor didn't match Conrad's usual standard, probably limited by Crockford's above. The $25 breakfast credit was only valid at three venues: The Kitchen (buffet), Suns Out Buns Out (knockoff Eggslut), and poolside Agave. The Kitchen is embarrassing as a Vegas buffet—only about 10 items plus fruit, charging $40/person. I used it at Suns Out Buns Out where there was a 20-minute wait. $25 got a breakfast sandwich and drink—the short ribs sandwich was really good. The hotel is brand new with pleasant scents throughout the lobby and casino, though rooms had a slight renovation smell. Next to the casino is a food court called Famous Foods with Asian street food—Japanese grilled skewers, dumplings, chow fun, beef noodles, baobing, etc. We ordered room service one evening and the staff waived everything at checkout. The 66th floor has Starlight Lounge with strip views, but minimum 2 drinks per person and overpriced drinks deterred us. The only frustration was service issues: body wash arrived empty, no coffee cups or capsules provided with the coffee machine despite several front desk calls. Capsules eventually arrived without cups. However, they generously sent a full sleeve of Nespresso capsules. Overall: hardware is indisputably excellent; service hasn't caught up yet due to recent opening; location is very remote; but the hotel has everything so no need to leave. Now it's included in FHR which offers $100 food credit + $30x2 FHR breakfast credit + $25x2 Diamond breakfast credit = $210 total, which seems great value if you find a cheap rate.

unmatched.author: bumblebee
unmatched.content: Did you ask the front desk about upgrades at Delano? FHR guarantees at least one room upgrade. I stayed twice in August - the first time I was upgraded directly to a Penthouse, the second time to a strip view corner suite.

unmatched.author: RedBlaze
unmatched.content: $100 credit used at Chica, restaurant was decent. $65 breakfast credit used at Grand Lux Cafe, dishes ordered lasted three meals for two people. Did not use early check-in and late check-out benefits.

unmatched.author: eye
unmatched.content: Welcome fruit and 2 bottles of water delivered to room. $100 credit used at Mott 32 for takeout, no reservation. $65 breakfast credit used at Grand Cafe.

unmatched.author: tibo
unmatched.content: FHR stay two nights get one free, $100 credit. Hotel has one restaurant with fusion Sino-Italian cuisine. CAD 65 tasting menu, pasta is decent. But with so many great options in Vancouver, don't waste a dinner allocation here. Breakfast is à la carte with same menu daily. Coffee ok, food skip. Excellent location, solid facilities, except restaurant.

unmatched.author: MKLDH
unmatched.content: Very good value, especially considering in-hotel spending CAD equates to 20% discount. Common areas slightly dated, room decor decent. Dinner and breakfast at hotel's only restaurant. Regular menu quality average, breakfast quality good ($30 USD x2 for any selection).

unmatched.author: brizyyy
unmatched.content: We arrived early. When dropping off luggage at the front desk, they only mentioned upgrading to a corner room. At actual check-in, we were finally upgraded to the hotel's largest suite. Hotel dining is ordinary.

unmatched.author: Oddinaaaaaa
unmatched.content: Probably because check-in wasn't until 4-5pm, most rooms were taken. We basically used all the credits for eating and drinking. Tableau doesn't open until around 8:30 so if booking remember to watch the time. Overall average, prices are expensive. Jardin is quite good—the view outside is open but relatively average, but the food is excellent! Lobster benedict is delicious, the pot-shaped small cake is incredibly sweet, very photogenic. The price is acceptable. A friend went to Urth Cafe and also found it quite sweet. I had milk tea, quite mediocre, felt my heart racing after drinking. For the SPA we did a classic facial with a friend. The facilities are modest and cramped, but the facial itself was very comfortable! Very satisfied! Vegas is so dry, the facial treatment was lifesaving. Wynn's casino was packed with people and smells like cigarettes...I'd rather gamble at the ping pang pong casino—it's old but doesn't smell bad...

unmatched.author: Sheriaty
unmatched.content: Stayed at Shangri-La Vancouver on 11/26/2021 via FHR prepay. Booked a superior king but received deluxe king. No hotel membership. Benefits: breakfast + $100 CAD food credit (confirmed at front desk). No welcome amenities visible, possibly due to having no elite status. Booked same morning for evening check-in so rushed. Requested crib wasn't available, added extra bed instead—uncomfortable bed, worse than sleeping on floor. Check-in/check-out times didn't allow using benefits. $100 credit not many places to use, but covered parking (about half). Only bought snacks. Should have had a drink. Waited ~30 minutes for main breakfast course after fruit smoothie, never arrived—child got bored so took it to go. Haven't received FHR survey yet. Overall stay acceptable, not used to high-end hotels.

unmatched.author: EricandK
unmatched.content: Booked 4 rooms with one card; rooms sold out that day, four rooms not on same floor. Two 1.5L bottles of mineral water on mini bar charged at $22 each—ridiculous. Used breakfast credit to pay at checkout. Dining credit valid at 120 MGM restaurants. Dinner at ground floor LUPO—sea bass decent, rest average, this restaurant not on the list. Limited lunch options on list, used at Bellagio Noodles—mediocre.

unmatched.author: Ccc1
unmatched.content: No views from the 10th floor. Credit cannot be applied to parking. Room showed no visible upgrades. Overall impression is the hotel was not generous with its benefits.

unmatched.author: ElectricSheep
unmatched.content: Hotel is located in the harbor area, very close to Little Italy downtown for dining. Through Amex Platinum prepay booking for two consecutive nights, receive $200 reimbursement. THC benefits provide an additional $100 credit. Since Globalist status already includes breakfast, the credit can be used for parking ($40/day) and hotel retail shopping. This property has many suites; Globalist members upgrade easily. Breakfast is decent by American standards—at least it's seated buffet service with staff assistance, and operates until 11am so can serve as brunch. However, not worth paying for: the bill shows $35 per person, which with tip and tax comes to about $85-90 for two people. Only worth visiting if breakfast is complimentary.

unmatched.author: kakatro
unmatched.content: Checked in at 9 PM; the Scenic View suite wasn't ready yet and was being cleaned. They gave us an additional $75 food credit. Combined with the $100 FHR credit, we had a great experience at the top-floor Rivea restaurant. The $30 breakfast credit could be used at Della Kitchen, which was quite good; we only paid $10 tip for breakfast in the end. At checkout, the $175 credit didn't apply correctly, but after some explanation they waived the remainder too, so we ended up with $178 in credits. The credit experience was excellent and the room was much better than the basic room we got at Park MGM later.

unmatched.author: lala
unmatched.content: 备注：

unmatched.author: lala
unmatched.content: 备注：

unmatched.author: lala
unmatched.content: Checked in after 9 PM. Requested an upgrade but only received a corner suite on the 7th floor. Since Rivea wasn't open and other restaurants were closing, we went to KUMI, a Japanese restaurant mixed with American and Korean cuisine. The food was decent—fried rice was good, the sushi meat wasn't very fresh but was edible and free anyway. Total came to $100. Overall satisfactory with decent portion sizes. The main issue: the corner suite's window had a ventilation leak beneath it, causing strong cold air during sleep. Used the breakfast credit at Della's Kitchen, which despite being a breakfast spot stays open until 2:30 PM. Went at lunch and the portions were large—a $20 burrito filled an entire plate, enormous but not particularly tasty. The coconut crème waffle was good, better than the pancake. At checkout, waited almost 10 minutes because there were few staff members and one was inexperienced, taking a long time to check in two other guests. When it was my turn to check out, my $100 FHR credit wasn't applied at all, and the staff didn't proactively show me the folio, wanting to directly charge my card. Only after I asked them to print the folio did I discover this. The staff was very inexperienced.

unmatched.author: goosekey
unmatched.content: Booked through THC. After entering Hyatt member number, it appeared directly in the Hyatt app. Confirmed SNP received: complimentary buffet breakfast (including tips) and $100 THC credit. Front desk initially said could not waive destination fee, but after negotiating with management they agreed to waive it. YMMV.

unmatched.author: lalaohlala
unmatched.content: Budget king upgraded to king with strip view. $65 breakfast credit (Grand Lux Cafe only), $100 FHR food credit. Dedicated Amex FHR staff check-in, very accommodating. Stayed 2 nights with same benefits on second check-in. Grand Lux Cafe breakfast generous and good value (portions large, flavor good, crowds after 10am). Tried Mott 32 (decent) and Matteo's pasta (mediocre), didn't get peking duck reservations.

unmatched.author: someleo
unmatched.hotel_name_en: Conrad at Las Vegas Resorts World
unmatched.content: $125 credit available for any hotel facilities; $66 breakfast credit per room usable at participating restaurants, tips excluded. Skipped checkout due to long line and received full statement, but resolved most charges by phone. Didn't get $15 in boba tea from the food court credited.

unmatched.author: hydralisk
unmatched.content: Part of Kessler Collection, a niche hotel brand with Southern mansion luxury aesthetic. Located beside Savannah's largest park (Forsyth Park), slightly removed from the commercial core (15-20 min walk). Hotel features various paintings and collectibles. Restaurant is in an authentic historic building, worth visiting. The $100 Amex credit can be used for resort fees and dining.

unmatched.author: wsgcjdtc
unmatched.content: Front desk arranged with manager to convert spa credit to dining credit (spa may be reopened, conversion unavailable next time). Completed welcome drink at Wynn lobby bar, additional drinks covered by dining credit. Pre-booked breakfast at Jardin in Encore tower—excellent taste. Used remaining dining credit at lobby cafe for takeout bread for national park trip breakfast.

unmatched.author: Jeffrey
unmatched.content: Breakfast buffet closed due to pandemic, in-room breakfast ordered instead, quality acceptable. $100 property credit usable at downstairs restaurants.

unmatched.author: easyandme
unmatched.content: Estepona is one of my favorite small towns in southern Andalusia—quiet, with beautiful scenery and charming streets. On my 2019 Spanish road trip, most stays were at state-run Paradores, but when passing through the Costa del Sol I stayed at this resort hotel in Estepona. I booked a standard budget room; on arrival it was full so the front desk upgraded one level, but it was still a standard room. The hotel has some age to it—clean facilities but the building overall looks rather dated. Balcony sea views were nice and the marble bathtub has good space. The pink exterior color—forget Royal Hawaiian, just stay at an authentic pink-walled Mediterranean resort. The hotel has many pools; the outdoor pools are large but unfortunately unheated. The garden path to the beach is perfect for leisurely strolls. Dinner was decent—grilled sirloin and grilled seabass. The highlight was watching authentic Andalusian Flamenco up close. The breakfast buffet was excellent with diverse options in a great setting, with flavors above standard. Estepona is a good stopover from Málaga to Seville, and I highly recommend this hotel if you're driving through and planning to stay.

unmatched.author: brokenarcher
unmatched.content:
Hotel: Vegas Conrad
Stay dates: 12/24-26
Booking method: FHR
Booked/actual room type: basic room / strip view King suite (58th floor)
Hotel membership: Aspire Diamond
Benefits: I stayed here before when it first opened, though not booked through FHR. In summary, this hotel is very generous and casual with credits—if you go over they waive it, and while various credits have restrictions, they'll manually override everything at the end, so use them freely.

$125 property credit works for everything except shopping. We used it at Genting Palace—overpriced but decent food...Aspire gives $50 daily for any restaurant...FHR gives $66 daily breakfast, only at The Kitchen, can order à la carte or buffet...Overall stay charged Genting Palace, food court, tiger sugar boba, kitchen breakfast. Final folio waived everything except tip.

unmatched.author: NuHuo
unmatched.content: Crockfords LXR Las Vegas. Stayed late January 2022. Booked Strip view King, received King suite. GOLD member. SNP benefits: $125 dining credit; $33 x 2 breakfast (only at The Kitchen); upgraded to suite but with poor view on 5th floor overlooking parking garage. Excellent front desk service. Lobby nearly empty while neighboring Conrad had 30 people in line.

unmatched.author: Rongye
unmatched.content: St. Regis service is exceptional. Upon arrival, personalized welcome envelopes with my name and room key were prepared, and the room had welcome fruit gifts (first time seeing welcome amenities in the US, very touching). The only downside is the lack of complimentary breakfast—FHR and Platinum benefits converted to $30 per person credit, which isn't much, but given the upgrade to an Astor suite I won't complain. The bathtub is huge and wonderful! I initially worried about the suite upgrade reliability, so I stacked a suite certificate, but the hotel confirmed FHR upgrade to Astor five days in advance, saving me the certificate. The property hardware is somewhat dated, but excellent service completely compensates. Highly recommended!

unmatched.author: MarriottBonvoy
unmatched.content: YMMV data point: No visible room upgrade from budget double room. 10th floor with no view. Food credit cannot be used for parking. No observable room upgrade. Overall impression: hotel was not generous.

unmatched.author: ZzzLi
unmatched.hotel_name_en: Foundry Hotel, Curio Collection by Hilton
unmatched.content: Booked during THC promotion at 25% off. The hotel is converted from an abandoned factory, very new and quite unique. Spacious room with good service. Interestingly, for a two-night stay, the breakfast ($60), parking ($28), and bar beverages ($10) from the second day were not charged on the final bill.

unmatched.author: Sm0421
unmatched.content: Good service, added rollaway bed for $40. Hotel suits young people but not families. Great view, children enjoyed it. Good room type. Initially considered booking wrap around suite with fountain view, but after observing the location decided this one was actually better. 23rd floor is perfect—any higher and you can't see clearly. 1:30 check-in, 2:30 room ready.

unmatched.author: Scott
unmatched.content: Experience detailed in blog post: Lost Civilization—Peru Machu Picchu and Inca Trail Travel and Hotel Experiences

unmatched.author: jefferyz
unmatched.content: Just normal FHR benefits, my partner had a good time

unmatched.author: MKLDH
unmatched.content: Excellent FHR value. Official website non-refundable rate comparable to FHR cancellable rate. Relais & Châteaux has no public member program, so earn 5x MR confidently. All-inclusive property so $100 credit only for spa. Unlike US hotels with spa credits requiring additional payment, $100 here gets 1 hour massage - relatively fair. Book massage immediately after check-in as therapist is not resident but travels from nearby city. Recommend 2nd tier Dawn/Dusk rooms (named for sunset/sunrise views) for chance to upgrade to Corner room like author did - Corner has both sunrise and sunset views, best room in hotel, $400/night more than 2nd tier.

g308272-d1654265.author: Eddie
g308272-d1654265.content: The best service among all three properties, far surpassing the Jing'an location. Handwritten cards and excessively generous welcome fruit. Flair has a nice view; without ordering alcohol, there's a Balinese appetizer platter with exceptional value. Jinyuan restaurant's flavors are average—possibly ordered the wrong dishes. The golden-threaded oatmeal shrimp was decent, but the crab course and silver ginkgo with fresh tofu were misses. The gym has plenty of equipment and excellent views of the three major landmarks. In addition to the main pool, there's a warm water massage pool.

g45963-d91703.author: Eddie
g45963-d91703.content: Only hotel where Amex Platinum card was verified. Self-checkout wasn't available at the time. Restaurant was mediocre. Granted 4pm checkout.

g45963-d91703.author: bumblebee
g45963-d91703.content: Bellagio is quite dated and feels rather average. However, it is the only MGM property with acceptable self-serve breakfast in August, and dining options are convenient.

g45963-d91703.author: Eddie
g45963-d91703.content: December 2020 Bellagio booked via FHR prepay, received points but not room night credits

g45963-d91703.author: patrickwang
g45963-d91703.content: Booked a beggar resort king and was upgraded to a renovated premier king. However, the premier king had no bathtub! After driving the scenic loop, we naturally wanted a bathtub, so we called to complain. Finally upgraded to a high floor fountain-view Queen. The room view was excellent, facing the fountain and Paris Hotel directly, but it wasn't renovated, and the facilities were a bit old. Plus, facing the Strip is actually very noisy... the fountain runs until midnight, the room soundproofing is poor, and neighbors playing music in the middle of the night woke us up. Breakfast credit was $60, but their food is very overpriced - just ordered a bit randomly and used it all up. The $100 dining credit can be used at almost all MGM restaurants (except Din Tai Fung). I was too lazy to walk so used it at Bellagio's buffet. Don't go there! Bellagio's buffet has minimal options and poor quality - it's like a $20 buffet (but with tax and tip it comes to over $50 per person). A $50+ buffet without even crab legs - can you believe it?

g45963-d91703.author: brokenarcher
g45963-d91703.content: The check-in staff wasn't familiar with FHR procedures. They completed the check-in before realizing it was FHR and only upgraded us after I asked—reluctantly upgraded to fountain view, though the room wasn't ready so they said they'd send an SMS. The staff just handed me the FHR benefits printout to read myself, which reflected a cold attitude (unlike the Wynn staff who explained the benefits line by line, and later a manager explained it again). After waiting 90 minutes without an SMS, I called and was told the room was ready. The phone staff kept emphasizing that upgrades and early check-in were upon availability. When I picked up the key, a different staff member was much friendlier and even upgraded me to a higher floor, but still no suite upgrade. The $30 breakfast credit was only usable at three places: Sadelle's (upscale breakfast) and two cafes. Sadelle's menu was overpriced—$30 wasn't enough. The cafes seemed mediocre, so we ended up at Wicked Spoon (Marriott members get priority, which was great). Later we used the credits at a cafe for coffee and ice cream. The breakfast options were limited regardless. The $100 food credit was much more flexible—usable at any MGM restaurant that allows room charges. The buffet looked disappointing at $50/person, so we ended up buying drinks poolside ($30+ for 32oz). Overall: location 5/5, service 3/5, FHR experience 4/5. The fountain view was nice—Channel 22 had live music.

g45963-d91703.author: kkzhou
g45963-d91703.content: Stayed 12/21-12/23. Booked FHR Prepay. Premier King upgraded to high floor fountain view. MGM Gold status from Caesar Diamond match. FHR credits used correctly, though food quality was mediocre. Used sandwich technique for upgrade, booked separately with partner's card, negotiated with front desk, kept room on second check-in.

g45963-d91703.author: lalaohlala
g45963-d91703.content: Budget king with no view. $60 breakfast credit (Sadelle's only, very crowded), $100 FHR food credit used for noodles/Cantonese (stir-fried beef hor fun with sesame sauce—not appealing). Very crowded overall. MGM Gold fast check-in line available but staff unhelpful, no room upgrade. Smallest room with no bathtub, bathroom missing door, poor water pressure. Wynn was better overall with fewer crowds.

g45963-d91703.author: wsgcjdtc
g45963-d91703.content: Used gold card for VIP check-in, upgraded to Strip View King in south wing of main tower (fountain view unavailable). Dining credit originally planned for Christmas Eve buffet, but buffet closed evenings—pivoted to pre-booked Lemongrass Thai at Aria with excellent taste, two people enjoyed $100 credit fully. Pre-booked breakfast at Saddles, reputed as best brunch in Las Vegas with genuinely excellent taste—strongly recommend pre-booking to avoid long walk-in waits.

g45963-d91703.author: NuHuo
g45963-d91703.content: Bellagio. Stayed late January 2022. Booked basic room, received fountain view. MGM GOLD member. SNP benefits: $100 MGM dining credit; $30-33 per person breakfast (max 2 people). Sandwich hack for $20 easily gets fountain view. MGM GOLD gets free parking.

g45963-d91925.author: Eddie
g45963-d91925.content: Weekend breakfast included brunch. Had three buffet meals until stuffed. Didn't want to eat king crab legs for a year afterward... (now I can again).

g45963-d91925.author: Eddie
g45963-d91925.content: January 2020 Aria booked via LHRC, received all Hyatt SNP benefits

g32655-d8678267.author: Eddie
g32655-d8678267.content: Breakfast was pretty good by American standards! Before the Rose Bowl, the hotel felt booked by OSU. Requested late checkout but didn't receive it.

g28970-d24799068.author: Eddie
g28970-d24799068.content: Excellent POV views. Generous portions—$100 for two was more than enough. Received personalized handwritten cards, dessert, and 4-hour late checkout. Overall very generous. If maximizing dining credit and willing to switch, you can alternate with the adjacent Willard Intercontinental.

g28970-d24799068.author: anon87878907
g28970-d24799068.content: The GM demonstrated exceptional obstructiveness; expected a premium surprise suite with connecting rooms but received a fragmented version instead. However, the hotel thoughtfully prepared photos and snacks for my cat. The vinyl record player in the room was broken. The rooftop restaurant offers views of the White House.

g45963-d15182489.author: bumblebee
g45963-d15182489.content: NoMad is inside Park MGM. Being nonsmoking may suit family travel with children, but rooms are very small.

g45963-d15182489.author: eye
g45963-d15182489.content: $100 credit used at MGM noodle restaurant, reservation required. $65 breakfast credit can only be used at Primrose Cafe. Didn't use full $100, remaining $10 directly deducted from resort fee.

g45963-d15182489.author: Jackey_j
g45963-d15182489.content: $100 credit can be used at 120+ MGM restaurant. $65 breakfast credit can only be used at Primrose Cafe. Didn't use full $100, remaining deducted from resort fee.

g189400-d206785.author: c94400129
g189400-d206785.content: Overall excellent stay. All-suite property with convenient location. Beautiful lobby fragrance, great fitness center, unlimited 250ml water bottles available (unlike certain other hotel chains where even Ambassadors only get two free bottles). Front desk initially didn't notice the FHR status and thought it was just regular Gold, but quickly apologized once discovered and attitude noticeably improved. Found a face mask in the trash can upon entry (a bit gross but was too lazy to call and complain). Cleaning supplies package on desk with masks, hand sanitizer, gloves. Amenities branded with Shanghai Tan fragrance, very nice. In-room dining quality good but portions small with 18% service charge already deducted. Otherwise nothing particularly remarkable. During checkout, in-room dining charges totaled $180 (Gold + FHR officially only covers $150) but given FHR VIP status, everything was fully covered. Final balance: $0. Wish I'd ordered more knowing it would all be reimbursed!

g60982-d114024.author: SIMNIENIE
g60982-d114024.hotel_name_en: The Royal Hawaiian, a Luxury Collection Resort
g60982-d114024.content:
Stay period: End of May 2021. Booking via FHR Prepay. Booked Garden View room because I was concerned a no-view room might not upgrade and would be disappointing. Actually received an upgrade to Ocean View. Held Gold hotel membership (from Platinum card).

SNP/Member benefits included: SNP, 5x points, 100 credit, 200 reimbursement, welcome gift, double breakfast, 4pm late checkout.

Check-in required showing and swiping the Platinum card for FHR benefits eligibility. I didn't bring the card used for booking since I'd just opened a new Platinum for opening spend, but the check-in agent allowed using the new card instead (same cardholder name) — a bit of an unusual data point.

Many online reviews mentioned the hotel feeling dated, but I found it quite nice. The interior decoration is retro-styled and the bathroom sink counter is small, but other furnishings are well-designed.

Service was excellent. The welcome gift was thoughtful and practical. The body wash effectively removed sunscreen (unlike at another property). The shared pool and beach with the adjacent Sheraton were high quality.

Post-pandemic, most good Waikiki restaurants require reservations, including this one. Unable to secure a dinner reservation, I ate lunch at the breakfast restaurant. Both breakfast and lunch were quite good.

g297672-d302891.author: Scott
g297672-d302891.content: Former royal palace now recognized as one of India's top hotels. Impeccable service. Rooms include private prayer chamber and butler service. Fresh fruit replaced during guest absence, flowers arranged at door each morning. Private pool available. Bath amenities feature natural aromatics.

g297672-d302377.author: Scott
g297672-d302377.content: Located across the lake from Oberoi Udaivilas. Former royal palace. Low occupancy during summer off-season. Lakeside pavilion features intricate blue stone inlay work inspired by Taj Mahal architecture—blue crystals inset into carved grooves in milky stone.

g304555-d302157.author: Scott
g304555-d302157.content: Favorite of the three palaces. Peacocks roaming grounds with jasmine fragrance throughout. Golf course with championship-quality greens. Over 200 years of history with meticulous architecture. Dining available indoors or outdoors with flute and drum performances in garden.

g34152-d115176.author: cchu1109
g34152-d115176.content: Resort fee covered by $100 credit. Negotiated upgrades before arrival and received several suite upgrades. Restaurant is reasonably priced. The hotel is genuinely beautiful—girlfriend couldn't stop taking photos. The $100 credit can be used at the gift shop for cigars, alcohol, clothing, and even valet service. Overall very satisfied. P.S. This was my first FHR stay.

g60763-d220243.author: anon87878907
g60763-d220243.content: Hotel name: W New York. Stay period: Holiday. Booking channel: MasterCard World Elite Concierge. Booked room type / Actual room type: Basic suite / Luxury duplex suite. Hotel membership: Unknown. Special notes: Once the first W hotel in the world, but has since been delisted.

g298555-d2682464.author: illusionwing
g298555-d2682464.content: $100 spa credit; cheapest option with tax was ~640 RMB. Requires reservation which can only be booked after check-in, so reserved for next morning. Pool is very small.

g294217-d2016610.author: KAPI
g294217-d2016610.content: All the benefits that should be provided were given, nothing to complain about. The $100 FHR credit can be used at restaurants, spa, and room service. The corner room view is particularly beautiful, and the hotel staff is very considerate.

g60890-d214890.author: intermilan
g60890-d214890.content:
Hotel Name: The Charles Hotel

Stay Period: August 2020

Booking Channel: FHR Pay Later

Booked Room Type / Actual Room Type: Basic room / Deans Suite

Hotel Membership: None

SNP/Member Benefits: None

Notes: College-style Charles Hotel Dean's Suite experience - unexpectedly upgraded to a suite. The rate was very affordable at the time with low occupancy. Probably harder to get now.

g45963-d675000.author: patrickwang
g45963-d675000.content: Booked a beggar room and was upgraded one level. Two people received $65 breakfast credit and $100 property credit. Breakfast only redeemable at Grand Lux Cafe and room service. Grand Lux Cafe has great quality, recommend the Short Rib Benedict. Used property credit at Matteo Italian restaurant in the hotel - overall very good, the pork belly was excellent, ravioli tasted like dumplings.

g60814-d13150813.author: 纳鲁的勇士
g60814-d13150813.hotel_name_en: Perry Lane Hotel, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g60814-d13150813.content: Great drinks and food. The only disappointment was that the rooftop bar closed too early.

g154912-d186815.author: Joeysocal
g154912-d186815.content:
Hotel: Fairmont Chateau Lake Louise

Stay period: September 2021, Labor Day Weekend

Booking channel: FHR Prepay

Booked room type/Actual room type: Economy room / Lakeview room (directly overlooking Lake Louise, absolutely stunning)

Hotel membership: NA

SNP/Membership benefits: NA / $100 Food credit, Free breakfast, 4PM check-out, discounted canoeing with priority line

Actually, my partner and I each booked one night. My night didn't have a room upgrade or early check-in, but we received a welcome gift (sparkling water and three desserts).

My partner's night didn't have a welcome gift, but the room was upgraded to the Lakeview Room directly overlooking Lake Louise—absolutely gorgeous.

The hotel restaurants: the Lakeview Lounge has a floor-to-ceiling window directly facing the lake, dubbed the "Window of the World" and perfect for social media. The Fairview Bar & Restaurant also has several slightly smaller windows with decent views. Both restaurants require advance reservations, especially Afternoon Tea which cannot be booked the same day.

Hotel guests receive discounted canoeing rates (CAD$99) and can skip the line with priority access. Some other guests who had waited 1+ hour were quite unhappy about this. Haha, sorry!

g52024-d12351447.author: z916055929
g52024-d12351447.hotel_name_en: The Duniway Portland, a Hilton Hotel
g52024-d12351447.content: $100 credit and $30x2 dual breakfast can be used as any credit including parking, tips, bar, breakfast. Breakfast is regular buffet with eggs, bread, sausage, yogurt, and coffee. Evening bar highly recommended, cocktails made to order.

g52024-d12351447.author: Oddinaaaaaa
g52024-d12351447.content: Overall a very pleasant stay. Booked two rooms with a friend—one through FHR and one directly on their website (Gold status wasn't matched at booking). At check-in, the front desk agent initially didn't offer an upgrade, but after asking, immediately upgraded both rooms to the premium king on the top floor. Not sure if this was due to Gold status. Parking was $33/day and covered by the credits. Breakfast credit was very economical at the downstairs restaurant—hard to spend the full $60 without alcohol. Surprisingly, gratuity was included in the meal cost, so aside from the room rate, there were no additional expenses. The FHR concierge sent an amusing invitation letter in a paper envelope. The room had a city view overlooking Pioneer Square with decent scenery. One miss: room service didn't arrive, likely due to New Year's Day, but with only a three-day stay it wasn't a big deal. Overall excellent value and highly recommended!

g297418-d15008481.author: happypants
g297418-d15008481.content: $100 credit available for any use, but restaurant food was mediocre. Platinum member 30% discount and $100 double dip YMMV.

g29092-d19842738.author: PPCkkt
g29092-d19842738.content: Room brand new, service very thoughtful, feels like one of the best serviced Marriott properties. Breakfast unremarkable, everything cold except oatmeal. Rooftop has Disney fireworks view on weekends. Convention center nearby convenient for meetings.

g29092-d19842738.author: 金卡会员
g29092-d19842738.content: Weekend at 9pm, fireworks visible from the balcony across from Disneyland. Unfortunately it rained in LA which is rare, and the rooftop bar was closed.

g29092-d19842738.author: Qorus
g29092-d19842738.content: Hotel Name: Westin Anaheim. Stay Period: May 2022. Booking Channel: AMEX FHR Prepaid. Booked economy king, received pool view king upgrade—only staying one night, didn't bother negotiating further. Hotel Membership: Member. FHR Benefits: Upgrade to pool view. $100 dining credit, used $50 towards dessert. Breakfast buffet, standard offering. Late checkout at 4pm: Required personal checkout, charged for parking after 4pm.

g45963-d91674.author: KaitouKiddo
g45963-d91674.content: Room looks a bit dated, but service is still decent. Received $100 spa credit which went unused. Breakfast provided $30 per person credit for restaurant downstairs.

g297463-d6034145.author: Eddie
g297463-d6034145.content: The restaurant has good flavors, nothing too hardcore. $100 credit was enough for three people. P6+ members get two-person happy hour but I didn't go, instead exchanged for extra breakfast. Chose welcome gift of 1000 points which was credited but haven't received yet. Asked multiple times for an eFolio, never received. After the first day, all three room cards stopped working. After the first repair at front desk, discovered all cards now light up all floors on the elevator.

g297427-d2270519.author: Eddie
g297427-d2270519.content: Originally booked two nights at Yalong Bay Marriott, but they refused the suite certificate even with rooms available and only offered deluxe garden view upgrade, so I cancelled. Happened to see FHR 800-150 offer and since I needed to move to Park Hyatt later with FHR's guaranteed 4PM checkout, switched to this hotel. Has poor reviews on Fei Ke but this stay was actually quite good. Wanted a pool-view room but it wasn't cleaned yet, manager brought me to see a garden-view suite with garden + mountain + sea view combo which was nice, so took the suite. Got extra breakfast for one person. Mini bar is free. Welcome fruit variety better than the previous two, though not as good as Shanghai Pudong Ritz-Carlton.

g13808515-d1879542.author: Jizz5566
g13808515-d1879542.content: 4 PM checkout, 2-person breakfast (set menu), $100 credit converted to TWD 2800 for restaurant dining. Pre-ordered signature roasted duck, added other menu items for in-room delivery. Prices are very luxury standard but with careful planning, only exceeded by 200+ NTD. Thought portions would be small but ended up too much to finish. Food was delicious. Also received 6-pack Uni mineral water for free. Service was excellent. Only downside: overcharged 2 bottles of Evian water at checkout, but after inquiry they said it was a member courtesy and refund is pending.

hilton_brands::cuncuqq.author: hermione
hilton_brands::cuncuqq.hotel_name_en: The Fives Downtown Hotel & Residences, Curio Collection by Hilton
hilton_brands::cuncuqq.content:
Booked 1 night with points + 2 nights THC. 35k HHonors at $150/night via THC, compared to under $100 on official website—markup is excessive. Received advance email about platinum upgrade, which also applied to later FHR bookings, so THC upgrade seems pointless. At check-in, must request the $100 credit or they'll pretend not to know about it.

Location very convenient, pool nice with ocean views from the height. Rooftop had octopus, tacos, and spinach ravioli—all good, octopus highly recommended. Room service ordered the most expensive filet mignon, worst steak I've ever eaten. Despite effort, couldn't finish the $100 credit.

g60950-d74515.author: xiaoxiaoX
g60950-d74515.content: Facilities quite old, maintenance extremely slow. AC broke at night, took two hours for someone to arrive and still wasn't fixed. Couldn't stay up waiting so had to ask for a small heater to get through the night.

g60950-d74515.author: IcyJ
g60950-d74515.content: Loews Ventana Canyon Resort, Tucson AZ. Stay period: Saturday. Booking channel: FHR. Booked room/Actual room: Standard/Luxury King. Hotel membership: None. SNP/Member benefits: $100 dining credit, $70 breakfast credit (before 11 AM). Received room upgrade to Luxury King, recently renovated and spacious with double soaking tub. Hotel is older but harmonious with surroundings. Excellent location - Sabino Canyon is only 8 minutes away. $100 credit is flexible. Flying V restaurant is good quality, $100 covers two entrees. Breakfast includes $70 credit; Canyon Cafe buffet is $23, can take food to go.

g60950-d74515.author: xlin2100
g60950-d74515.content: Lobby renovation has no impact, wouldn't have noticed if not mentioned. Only downside was no room upgrade. Food is mediocre, nothing special. Plenty of good restaurants nearby, Rocksville is just 20 minutes away. Hotel dining is good for drinks and appetizers. Room was criticized by partner - balcony has no view and is somewhat dark. But I felt it was good value. After FHR reimbursement, out-of-pocket was only $30, can't ask for more.

g60950-d74515.author: hydralisk
g60950-d74515.content: Room rate $300+. The front desk staff were very warm and directly upgraded us to a suite (which is actually two rooms combined). $100 dining credit + $70 breakfast credit. After the $200 FHR credit, it was essentially free. The front desk also prepared welcome juice and handmade chocolate. The resort is located in the foothills east of Tucson (Ventana Canyon). Inside the resort there's a small trail with a waterfall. Outside the resort is a challenging hiking trail that many people climb - about 7 miles round trip, 3-4 hours total, highly recommended.

g33676-d115904.author: tobit
g33676-d115904.content: Early in the morning I was told the room was ready when I wanted to store luggage, so I didn't care about the suite. Midway through the stay I discovered resort fees on the bill and asked the front desk. They gave a bunch of unreliable explanations but eventually agreed to email the manager, after which the resort fee was waived. The hotel hardware is decent but service is poor. The AC and bathtub in the room were broken, but repairs came fairly quickly and weren't a major issue—fixed in about 30 minutes. Dining was terrible. At breakfast with only 20% occupancy we waited 40 minutes for food; on the last day I found an insect in blackberries. Weekend breakfast offered buffet or à la carte (two menus), but the host said buffet only today. We were fine either way wanting buffet—we thought they were too busy. But once inside we saw previous guests eating à la carte food (what I had ordered the day before), and then guests arriving after us also started ordering à la carte. The impression wasn't great.

g33676-d115904.author: kruzer
g33676-d115904.content: I requested a suite upgrade before arrival but was declined. Instead, they offered a twin room with view and balcony, which was accepted. During check-in, shopping for supplies took so long I couldn't use the noon check-in benefit. The front desk staff were excellent—recognizing the complexity of the mixed FHR and points booking, they waived parking and resort fees entirely. Combined FHR and Globalist breakfast credit of $70 can be used for à la carte or buffet ($35 each). The first two days I couldn't finish the à la carte allowance and packed a lot away. The $100 credit covered a massage gun rental and dinner. Oddly, many charges didn't appear on my folio, and checkout cost less than expected—no additional charges appeared later. Very mysterious. Overall recommended, quite different from the review above. The gondola wasn't open when I left, but shuttle service made it manageable.

g60678-d7910276.author: Dddbx
g60678-d7910276.content: Only FHR option in my town. Their steakhouse is excellent. Used $100 credit at their Thai restaurant this time—flavors were delightful, very overpriced. Other dining areas are average.

g45963-d1123368.author: EricandK
g45963-d1123368.content: Booked 4 rooms with one card. $25 mini bar/welcome credit only available first day. Confirmed with front desk that $100 SPA credit is usable at dining. Ate at Wazuzu—average, pineapple fried rice decent, seafood noodles had plenty of seafood but noodles seemed undercooked. Jardin had good ambiance and decent food. Only ate breakfast once; at checkout, front desk proactively applied second day breakfast credit to dining. Ended up only tipping on two meals.

g45963-d1123368.author: lalaohlala
g45963-d1123368.content: Budget king upgraded to king with strip view. $60 breakfast credit, $100 spa credit (changed to F&B upon request, YMMV). Used credits at Jardin and in-room dining. Jardin ambiance decent but overpriced.

g29748-d17692337.author: Charon_pitt
g29748-d17692337.content: New Jersey beachside resort. Staff very friendly. Restaurants above average, cocktails excellent, great common areas. Rooms large and new with free parking.

g1183491-d1534591.author: 罗伯特
g1183491-d1534591.content: Located in Vail town with valet parking only. Breakfast was excellent with creative offerings like salmon skin ice cream rolled in crepes with honeycomb honey. Very European style and charm with historic character but not outdated. Service was exceptional - attentive but unobtrusive. Requested ice at front desk and it was already in the bucket when arriving at the room.

g308272-d1945926.author: herebjldn
g308272-d1945926.content: Used 2021 FHR $200 credit. Room rate was approximately $240 after tax. Used $100 credit for a bottle of red wine - better than nothing but mediocre quality. Front desk only saw it as an Expedia booking with special privileges and did not recognize it as FHR. Room facilities were acceptable but showed signs of age.

g297415-d17460833.author: herebjldn
g297415-d17460833.content: Used 2021 FHR $200 credit. Room rate was approximately $330 after tax. $100 credit converts to 650 RMB and can be used for dining including the rooftop bar, but taxes and service charges are separate. Located in Shenzhen city center with exceptional service. However, room space is somewhat cramped and some room types may have construction crane obstructions in the view.

g60956-d288617.author: goosekey
g60956-d288617.content: Turndown service was provided. Service throughout the hotel was exceptional - every staff member greeted us with a nod and smile. Direct access to River Walk downstairs, just a few steps to the River Walk cruise boarding area. Location is excellent. Highly recommended for anyone visiting San Antonio.

g1479123-d21092409.author: Mars
g1479123-d21092409.content: Front desk attendant was beautiful, sweet-voiced and helpful. I booked two nights with two platinum cards, and she said she'd arrange everything—no need to check in/out, and she's reset tomorrow's $160 credit in the system. Another guest asked about children and received tickets to a nearby kids' amusement park. Welcome amenities included a bottle of red wine and snacks (snacks quality was questionable, trail mix was decent). Valet parking attendant was good-looking but service level was mediocre. Room overall satisfactory, though appeared to have bugs or mosquitoes—didn't catch any but got bitten all over.

g60864-d120852.author: Rongye
g60864-d120852.content: Used $100 credit for dinner—taste was decent, two people ordering one entree each plus half a dozen oysters came to $99. Breakfast was excellent—honestly the best hotel breakfast I've had in America! While not abundant in variety, everything was well-executed and I was very satisfied. However, I must complain about hotel service. The hotel emailed asking about special requests on the morning of check-in, and I requested turndown service before 9pm. They confirmed via email. Yet from 8pm check-in until 11:30pm, nobody came to turn down the bed—finally near sleep time someone came up with a few chocolates. Frankly, quite disappointing. Also, the hotel pool is surprisingly small—not sure if that's typical for Ritz-Carlton? In summary, except for breakfast, this Ritz had nothing that felt worth the value. At $450 total minus $200 reimbursement, I still don't think it was worth it. Not really recommending you use credit here.

g60864-d120852.author: ZL10
g60864-d120852.content: FHR provided $100 credit and $60 breakfast. Overall excellent experience with good front desk service that promptly upgraded to city view. Learned that the $100 credit could be used to purchase Marriott gift cards, which I leveraged to get additional Amex offers. Visited Davenport Lounge for drinks in the evening with live jazz band and dancing (everyone dressed formally except me in a t-shirt). Breakfast at M Bistro was a weekend buffet with coffee, tea, juices, local specialty beignets, and an omelet station with customizable toppings.

g57415-d819665.author: silentmovie
g57415-d819665.content: $100 credit, 4pm guaranteed checkout. Resort fee: This property's resort fee includes valet parking. Getting the resort fee waived through Globalist status also waives valet parking—otherwise paid stays typically don't get free parking. Communication was needed, though their text message system is quite convenient. They readily waived the resort fee. Breakfast: Also waived through Globalist status, but required communication. The property is in early ski season, which is why pricing isn't too bad—almost nothing is open. Only one café is operating at lunch. Food pricing is extremely high; we spent over $50 for cafe items. Dinner is currently in the conference hall, also very expensive. That said, ski access is convenient.

g60726-d84023.author: xlin2100
g60726-d84023.content: Platinum provided $60 breakfast credit plus $100 credit for other uses. Booked back-to-back for two days using two cards, both under my name, but the front desk split it into two reservations. As a result, I received $160 in credits each day, totaling $320, and could order anything from the hotel. The only requirement was to go down to the front desk on the second day to reactivate the room key to keep the same room.

g60726-d84023.author: ArrivingAgain
g60726-d84023.content: $60 breakfast credit per room, $100 usable anywhere, welcome gift was macarons. Found candy wrappers in minibar on arrival—called front desk and received 5000 Marriott points. Some facilities renovated, overall a bit dated. For a $280/night FHR, overall experience was satisfactory. $30 per person breakfast was adequate. On Sunday discussed possibility of switching breakfast to brunch.

g60713-d81450.author: kakaru
g60713-d81450.content: Ritz-Carlton San Francisco. Stay period: December Saturday to Monday. Booking channel: FHR Prepay. Booked room/Actual room: Club Level King Size/Club Level King Size (corner room with no view). Hotel membership: Marriott Silver Elite. SNP/Member benefits: Double occupancy bookings earned one night SNP and two nights Platinum status, but no member benefits were applied. Hotel doesn't value FHR customers. Only basic FHR-required benefits honored: 4pm checkout, $100 dining credit (total $200). Front desk tried to deny the $200 credit on second check-in due to alternating bookings. Complimentary upgrade promised in FHR terms has fine print about hotel discretion, so it was denied. Most frustrating: they assigned a room with only interior courtyard view when street-view rooms are available - feels like a downgrade. Club lounge was extremely crowded on Saturday.

g32815-d23166021.author: TravelLove
g32815-d23166021.content: Seabird Resort Oceanside. Stay period: 12/16-12/19. Booking channel: FHR. Booked room/Actual room: Partial Ocean View King → Ocean View King. Hotel membership: None. SNP/Member benefits: Stay 2 Get 1 promotion (counts as two Hyatt nights), $40 breakfast credit per person per day, $125 dining credit. Located between LA and SD, close to Carlsbad - a beautiful but not crowded coastal city. Newly opened hotel (6 months old), excellent facilities and service, part of Destination by Hyatt. $125 was perfect for two adults and a small child for one dinner. Downside: nearly $50 resort fee and valet-only parking (or $5/day at public lot next to hotel, but need to repay at midnight).

g60898-d513075.author: kevinzhang99
g60898-d513075.content: Standard FHR benefits: early check-in, 4pm checkout, $100 credit for dining, self-parking available, and $30×2 breakfast credits. The hotel is currently undergoing renovation with the front desk relocated to an inconspicuous location—I missed it on my first visit. Due to the renovation, the restaurant is very small, feeling like a temporary setup in the main lobby. Limited menu options, but the $11 calamari platter was a pleasant surprise. The room is standard—neither new nor old. Good location with Lenox shopping mall nearby.

g60713-d115617.author: 没姐官配精灵
g60713-d115617.content: Standard FHR benefits: early check-in, 4pm checkout, $100 credit for dining (restaurant, bar, or room service). Breakfast for two is $60 (room service excludes tip; can grab-to-go from restaurant counter but remember to mention FHR breakfast benefit or you may encounter a system issue). Garden Court is very popular—reserve ahead or expect 1+ hour wait. Valet-only parking at $72/day, expensive but unlimited in/out, totaling around $200. Pied Piper's burger and fries are excellent—highly recommended. Final rate after $200 Amex credit was just $313 after tax.

g60713-d115617.author: anon62168739
g60713-d115617.content: Excellent location—airport accessible via direct BART. Historic property (100 years), but rooms feel modern. FHR benefits: sea salt caramel chocolate, $100 dining credit, $60 daily breakfast credit. Dining options include Pied Piper, Garden Court, and a to-go counter. Food quality average overall. Pied Piper: salad and fried chicken—chicken was extremely salty, not recommended. Garden Court breakfast only: buffet ($39), decent atmosphere but book ahead with concierge. Afternoon tea supposedly famous but unavailable. Overall good value—room $300 plus Platinum reimbursement and dining credits made stay nearly free, but food quality disappointing.

g60713-d115617.author: naive_hk_journalist
g60713-d115617.hotel_name_en: Palace Hotel, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g60713-d115617.content: Booked last minute that morning. Received $100 dining credit and $60 breakfast credit. Checked in at 10pm, could only use all credits for breakfast. Ate at Garden Court with 2 people for $120, tip also covered by credit.

g60956-d108571.author: aprilyuyu
g60956-d108571.content: Many posts cover major city FHR properties, so I'm sharing San Antonio. The $100 credit can be used for anything except resort fees and room rate. Didn't have time to try the spa or golf. Scenic location on a hillside with great air quality.

g60898-d86260.author: jerryzzyy
g60898-d86260.content: FHR stay on 12/24 with $100 FHR credit and $80 breakfast credit, 4pm checkout, no welcome amenity. Budget room with no upgrade. This Ritz doesn't compare to Marriott except for slightly better toiletries and linens. Breakfast required waiting in queue, room service unresponsive—waited nearly 1.5 hours for room service.

g41562-d17469241.author: Max_CC
g41562-d17469241.content: $100 FHR credit, $80 breakfast credit, 4pm checkout (left at noon). Premier King upgraded to Harbor View, no welcome amenity. Overall decent experience—attentive front desk, free parking, no vaccine/mask requirements. Didn't visit casino due to cold weather outside. Breakfast mediocre and low value. Red 8 Chinese restaurant quality poor (overpriced, small portions, American-style Chinese, appears to reuse leftover food—seafood fried rice was mostly plain rice). Better value options exist unless using FHR benefits and visiting casino.

g41562-d17469241.author: lfan1992
g41562-d17469241.content: Had Chinese food with Peking duck. The quality was better than expected. Not worth the full price, but with $30-40 off after credits, it was quite reasonable. We ordered char siu, roasted duck, stringy green beans, and beef fried dumplings. The duck pancakes were too sticky, the char siu lacked flavor, and the dumplings lacked sauce. Funny story: I was about to order the Peking duck combo with duck congee and duck salad, when I ran into the Chinese concierge manager who subtly hinted we should avoid this—it's a tourist trap. Fortunately my partner caught on. The manager recommended ordering items separately from another page instead, and later walked by our table to give us a thumbs up when he saw us enjoying our meal.

g28970-d84090.author: 谢志光
g28970-d84090.content: Junior suites were available for upgrade when checking in, but FHR + Hyatt Diamond didn't secure a room upgrade. Front desk negotiation unsuccessful; didn't want to push too hard. Disappointed but compensated somewhat with the Blue Duck restaurant credit.

g55711-d21296495.author: 谢志光
g55711-d21296495.content: $125 credit usable for valet only, with two restaurants excluded - one doesn't accept walk-ins, so credit could only be applied to room service. Didn't get a proper meal. Poor experience.

g60750-d80668.author: siuu
g60750-d80668.hotel_name_en: The US Grant, a Luxury Collection Hotel, San Diego
g60750-d80668.content: $100 credit, $75 breakfast. Stayed mainly to use up annual FHR credit. No room upgrade provided. Front desk service was average. Standard room. Restaurant was mediocre in the evening but decent in the morning - the avocado toast was good and portions were generous.

g60750-d80668.author: Scott
g60750-d80668.hotel_name_en: The US Grant, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g60750-d80668.content: Experience detailed in blog post: Sunny Days—California Route 1 Road Trip and Surrounding Hotel Experiences

g60750-d80668.author: Martini
g60750-d80668.content: Total 3 nights: first night points redemption, next two nights separate FHR bookings. Points redemption upgraded 1 level due to Titanium, but FHR bookings unchanged. First night used suite certificate but no upgrade. Small rooms with European/American decor. Titanium provides $20+ breakfast or points choice. FHR provides $100 credit + $75 breakfast/in-room dining. Excellent value: $400+ total, $200 reimbursable, $100 credit for dinner, $75 for breakfast/lunch in-room. Downside: possibly missed tipping housekeeping one night - she knocked at 9am next morning, possibly intentionally.

g297458-d23548566.author: ylxzzg
g297458-d23548566.content: New hotel with no room upgrade. Front desk staff unhelpful and clueless, very poor experience.

g60864-d111970.author: ZL10
g60864-d111970.hotel_name_en: The Roosevelt New Orleans, A Waldorf Astoria Hotel
g60864-d111970.content: FHR provided $100 credit and $60 breakfast; Diamond gave an additional $50. The lobby had ornate lighting but was cramped with many tourists taking photos. Front desk was cold and unhelpful, refusing to upgrade even with FHR and elite status, claiming the next category was fully booked. Returned late from sightseeing and didn't have time for dinner, so the next morning used the $100 credit to buy Hilton gift cards. Breakfast at Fountain Lounge was a buffet with more variety than Ritz but no beignets. Only free coffee and tea; used some of the $50 Diamond credit for mimosa and juice. After buffet, went to nearby Teddy's Cafe with remaining credit and ordered spicy and sweet wings that were surprisingly good. Overall, the Waldorf service was poor and rooms were old. The room phone only connected to concierge who left early; I had to go downstairs to the front desk just to ask for tissues. For a New Orleans FHR choice between these two, I'd recommend the Ritz.

g28970-d84118.author: qgao8
g28970-d84118.content: Used the $100 credit for room service dinner with excellent service. The $67 breakfast credit was used for brunch at Greenhouse. Check-in at 10am, received a bathtub room on a high floor as requested, though the view wasn't special. Stayed until 3pm the next day. Service was exceptional compared to major chain five-star hotels. Highly recommended!

g60956-d99423.author: kkymc
g60956-d99423.hotel_name_en: The St. Anthony, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g60956-d99423.content: SNP benefits apply even with FHR prepaid bookings.

g52024-d1144098.author: koto1108
g52024-d1144098.hotel_name_en: The Nines, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g52024-d1144098.content: Overall unsatisfactory experience, host expressed disappointment. Downtown Duniway unavailable, other IHG property too far away. Pros: Downtown Portland location, walking distance to Apple Store and shops, credit covers standard 20% dining gratuity. Cons: Only valet parking available at $52 (credit doesn't apply), opted for Smart Park 24/7 lot two blocks away at $10/day. Food mediocre, room design uninspired, lacks 5-star luxury feel—feels like 4-star low-end or 3-star high-end, has gym but no pool. Large group of athletes checked in next day, entire floor reeked of marijuana, disgusting. Would not stay without $200 credit reimbursement.

g187457-d228552.author: MarriottBonvoy
g187457-d228552.hotel_name_en: Hotel Maria Cristina, A Luxury Collection Hotel
g187457-d228552.content: Really? In short, it tries too hard to be like a Marriott-branded hotel—very standardized, like staying at just any random Ritz or Regis in America, boring. It's a shame because Hotel Maria Cristina is the most famous century-old five-star hotel in San Sebastian. I expected to feel its heritage, a unique culture and flavor, but found none. The hardware and software are decent—clearly all staff take pride in their work and rooms are impeccably maintained, like new. But there was a major oversight: I had a restaurant reservation, only to be told the night before that the restaurant was closed at the reserved time. We had to order room service instead, which was terrible—the fish was oversalted and arrived in an airplane meal aluminum container. Breakfast was unremarkable. The biggest problem remains that it tries too hard to be a Marriott-branded hotel—it's just a random American five-star experience. Why do this? The desk drawers have no paper or pen, just a Book of Mormon. Completely off. Not an escape-immediately situation, but absolutely no reason to book.

g60763-d675616.author: MarriottBonvoy
g60763-d675616.content: I recommend a hotel to friends visiting San Sebastian called Lasala Plaza Hotel. I originally just wanted to use my CSP UR portal to cover it (considered a four-star hotel), but it was a wonderful surprise—it directly led to booking FHR for the next day. The hotel is a 3-5 minute walk from all the good taverns in the old city, a 2-minute walk to touristy beach attractions, yet quiet and hidden in a small alley. I booked a historic corner room which turned out to be more like a junior suite. After UR portal reimbursement it was only $180, compared to Maria Cristina's $400 for a budget room that same day. The hotel team is young but very professional, accommodating any request. The bathroom has a bidet toilet seat. In short, come to Lasala Plaza Hotel! No matter how far you are, you must come! Please!

g34515-d123528.author: intermilan
g34515-d123528.content: Contributing something not yet seen on the forum. This time in Orlando I experienced all FHR hotels except Four Seasons. Overall this one provided the best value. Planning to write a detailed post about it.

g45956-d84627.author: K-L837
g45956-d84627.content:
Just stayed this weekend:

Hotel: Hyatt Regency Lake Tahoe Resort
Stay period: weekend
Booking method: FHR Prepay
Booked/actual room type: King Bed (basic room) / 2 Double (was offered upgrade to King Bed with balcony but had to wait; after sitting in traffic all morning, partner decided to take the 2 Double that was ready)
Hotel membership: none
Benefits: $100 dining credit, breakfast for two, noon check-in, 4pm check-out (was told since checking out Sunday, 4:30-5pm no problem. If skiing nearby, most mountains close lifts at 4pm, so this is quite useful).

Hotel facilities are average, soundproofing on room doors is terrible. Limited restaurant options; the best one, Lone Eagle Grill (across from hotel), was fully booked since we didn't reserve. Limited menu options at hotel restaurants.

Update at 10pm: absolutely avoid rooms with connecting doors—extremely poor soundproofing. Neighbors' conversations carry through clearly, as does TV noise. Hotel has quiet hours starting at 9pm; if you're loud they'll send security up to ask you to keep it down.

g45956-d84627.author: wen666
g45956-d84627.content: Booked with teammate using two Amex Platinum cards for one night each. Front desk merged bookings and upgraded to the most expensive room type for over $2000 per night without requiring a room change.

g60763-d93607.author: TrashPanda
g60763-d93607.content:
Hotel: Loews Regency New York
Stay period: New Year's Eve 2021-2022 (12/31/2021 - 1/3/2022)
Booking method: FHR Prepaid
Booked/actual room type: Premium King / Studio King
Hotel membership: Loews YouFirst Loyalty program (white card, doesn't seem to be registered)
Benefits: $100 FHR Amenity + $70 breakfast (actually seems like we ate more than a few meals and didn't get charged extra?)

Remarks: Felt pretty good. Last-minute New Year's stay in NYC; FHR had a "stay 2 nights get 1 free" promotion so 3 nights total cost about $1.7k including tax and tips. Arrived at 10am and they said room was ready or we could wait for a Studio. Went out for lunch, came back at noon for check-in. Room is huge for Manhattan. Has bathtub, shower, sofa, chairs, microwave and second mini fridge in entryway. Hotel is a bit old but has interesting character. Free breakfast was good. The $100 dinner credit meal was mediocre. Seems like all Loews hotels offer the same FHR treatment?

g154943-d12619570.author: jzthinkgeek
g154943-d12619570.content: JW Marriott Parq Vancouver. Stayed January 2022 on a weekday. Booked King room, received King with water view. Marriott Gold member. SNP benefits: $100 USD credit for restaurant, parking, or SPA; $30 USD credit per person for breakfast (all converted to CAD at exchange rate). The $100 credit for parking was excellent, and SPA credits can be used on skincare products. Earned normal Marriott points and night credits.

g50207-d95213.author: MostlyHarmless
g50207-d95213.content: The Ritz-Carlton, Cleveland. Stayed one weekend in February 2022. Booked basic room, received basic room. Marriott Titanium member. SNP benefits: $100 credit usable only at the lobby bar (hotel's only restaurant). Dinner was salty and unappetizing. Breakfast equally poor. Titanium member received beverage voucher at same bar. No room upgrade. Hotel in old mall on 3rd floor with deteriorated parking lot. Poor cleanliness: opened cotton swabs in bathroom, dirty towel with visible stains, empty coffee capsules. No turndown service. Toiletries were mixed brands. Selected late checkout via app and confirmed at check-in, but was knocked on at 12:30pm to check out. Bill incorrectly charged valet (not used) and forgot breakfast credit. US Ritz-Carlton properties generally disappointing.

g60978-d20949649.author: TrashPanda
g60978-d20949649.content: Nice hotel overall. Recommended for friends on the East Coast who don't want to deal with complications. Off-season rates around $300+ per night.

g187497-d12941693.author: Chris123
g187497-d12941693.content: Booked this hotel after seeing the 25% discount brought the rate to $267—excellent value. Very satisfied with the stay. Convenient location, all nearby attractions walkable, three metro lines downstairs, airport bus stop 5 minutes walk away. Rooftop bar with perfect views of Sagrada Familia. First-class facilities and enthusiastic, accommodating service. Recommended for those visiting Barcelona for tourism or football matches.

g28970-d84026.author: RiceLord
g28970-d84026.content: Breakfast is buffet-style. Note that room service automatically adds 22% gratuity. The Watergate scandal room is on the second floor - when we checked in, it was already occupied, probably in high demand.

g55711-d1113787.author: ethanycx
g55711-d1113787.content: $42 valet parking and $120 brunch were fully reimbursed. $100 credit can be used on any room charges except the room rate (even to purchase hotel robes). Breakfast credit can be used for in-room service. The hotel restaurant is excellent, and there's complimentary house car service within downtown (< 3 miles). It seems smaller, independent FHR properties tend to offer better perks than chain hotels.

g60878-d281828.author: brokenarcher
g60878-d281828.content: The hotel is a bit dated. Even with the suite upgrade, the room is still relatively small with a small window overlooking nothing. The coffee machine isn't Nespresso—it's a fully manual Keurig, which is underwhelming. The hotel restaurant, Shuckers (oyster-themed), only opened weekends due to COVID. I was curious how to use the $100 credit if visiting on weekdays—do you have to order room service? The restaurant is dated and dimly lit, not my style. My partner and I ordered shareables (mostly seafood) and clam chowder since we planned to go to Pike Place later. The food was decent, nothing to write home about. Prices are definitely expensive. It's puzzling that most diners seemed to be guests at the hotel—were they coordinating a gathering? Breakfast was in the lobby, standard without highlights. Overall, this hotel isn't for me. Given its age, renovation can't change the layout; its historical significance outweighs the guest experience.

iprefer_points::CHSHB.author: ZzzLi
iprefer_points::CHSHB.content: The hotel was built in 2018, brand new, with excellent service. Very busy during spring break, no room upgrade provided.

amex_thc::Nevada-US/Las-Vegas/The-Veneti.author: Sm0421
amex_thc::Nevada-US/Las-Vegas/The-Veneti.content: Asked about premium queen (much larger) but required $130 per night surcharge, decided not to upgrade. Kids said this was the worst hotel of the three (Cosmopolitan and Marriott Vacation Club 2-bedroom were the others). The Venetian is okay. Next time in Vegas will try Hilton Grand Vacation (in Planet Hollywood) 2-bedroom for family travel. Size is most important, refrigerator and washing machine would be nice to have, everything else doesn't matter.

g32684-d78172.author: Scott
g32684-d78172.content: Experience detailed in blog post: Sunny Days—California Route 1 Road Trip and Surrounding Hotel Experiences

g32684-d78172.author: Scott
g32684-d78172.content: Experience detailed in blog post: Sunny Days—California Route 1 Road Trip and Surrounding Hotel Experiences

g294314-d304538.author: Scott
g294314-d304538.hotel_name_en: Palacio Del Inka, a Luxury Collection Hotel
g294314-d304538.content: Experience detailed in blog post: Lost Civilization—Peru Machu Picchu and Inca Trail Travel and Hotel Experiences

g56003-d98952.author: Rongye
g56003-d98952.content: Booked price $733, honestly not great value. The hotel itself is excellent with new facilities, good service, and convenient location, but $700 is still too expensive. No complimentary breakfast buffet; received $60 breakfast credits for two people. We ended up spending $90 including tax and tip to eat adequately. Had planned to use the $100 F&B credit for lunch, but the only option that day was $145 per person buffet, so didn't use it. However, it did cover the tax and tip from breakfast, so acceptable overall. The room view was quite nice! Could see the Toyota Center.

g32655-d224168.author: DELTA
g32655-d224168.content: Very generous—directly upgraded to studio suite, room was very spacious.
