Theoretically only one category upgrade. $100 credit plus $20 daily dining credit per day was about right for two people's breakfast. Hotel was very generous and service was excellent, only issue was forgetting to deduct credit at checkout and had to go back to front desk to fix it.

g155033-d155587.author: jm1990
g155033-d155587.content: Standard American buffet breakfast. Required swiping Platinum card at check-in. Received a letter and a Fudge Bar as welcome gift.

g155025-d183286.author: jm1990
g155025-d183286.content: Hotel under renovation with car service to room entrance. Private balcony, luxury amenities, slippers provided. Three dinner menus: vegetarian tasting menu (~$125), meat tasting menu (~$130), three-course meal ($85). Chose the affordable option—excellent food, no disappointments. Appetizer: halibut. Main: beef steak. Dessert: chocolate. Breakfast options were more limited.

g294013-d15134798.author: joxu
g294013-d15134798.content: Ultra-luxury desert resort in Abu Dhabi. Dinner buffet $199 per person—received $100 credit, front desk suggested only $15 out-of-pocket. Second night dinner (~$200 for two) seemingly waived; bill pending. Exceptionally courteous front desk upgraded room to premium suite with private pool. Complimentary, abundant breakfast.

g188052-d236358.author: Yeson
g188052-d236358.content: Great experience with excellent restaurant food and particularly enthusiastic service. Recently traveled to Switzerland on business and found this FHR in Bern at around 500 CHF, which felt like a perfect use of FHR points. The hotel is reportedly operated by the Swiss government with excellent views and location, and is not part of any hotel loyalty program. In short: highly recommended! Unfortunately time was tight so didn't use early check-in or late check-out, just enjoyed the meals. The FHR 100 credit was issued directly as a coupon and used when settling the bill. Upgraded to a suite at check-in.

g60654-d8684459.author: speed
g60654-d8684459.hotel_name_en: Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina
g60654-d8684459.content: The hotel rooms are a bit dated, but the environment is great and suitable for families with kids. There are lots of koi fish in the resort, and the doormen feed them. The breakfast buffet is nice, but the other restaurants are mediocre.

g60713-d1175274.author: Ranilv
g60713-d1175274.content: Fairmont San Francisco, stayed midweek weekday in May 2022 for 1 night via FHR Prepay. Booked queen, upgraded to king with city view. No hotel loyalty status. Standard FHR benefits: $100 dining credit, $90 breakfast credit, complimentary dessert in room, and approved late checkout request. Overall felt very good. Booked late using 20k points + $270. Points returned as $200 statement credit. Two meals exceeded the credits by ~$30 with tip, but FHR's $200 reimbursement arrived within a few days.

g155033-d209426.author: evanzzq
g155033-d209426.content:
Hotel: Auberge Saint-Antoine (Quebec City)

Stay period: May 16-17, 2022

Booking channel: FHR Prepay

Booked/Actual room type: Comfortable Queen / Luxury King (upgraded but the room is still quite small)

Benefits: $120 CAD dining credit used for dinner, but since the meal itself was expensive ($110 CAD prefix) I still had to pay extra. Breakfast quality was excellent. 4pm checkout.

g297988-d1210688.author: COVID-19
g297988-d1210688.content: Hotel Name: Argos in Cappadocia. Stay Period: May 2022. Booking Channel: AMEX FHR Prepaid. Booked Deluxe, received Suite upgrade (FHR offers three room types: Stone $405, Deluxe $524, Suite $881—I booked Deluxe to qualify for suite upgrade if available). Hotel Membership: Non-member. FHR Benefits: One-level upgrade to suite. Complimentary breakfast buffet, not notably different from typical Cappadocia cave hotels. $100 dining credit usable for one lunch or dinner for two people, excluding alcohol and tips. $100=1600 lira, covers five filet mignon dishes—restaurant manager waived overage charges. Outstanding service made us leave 100 lira cash tip. Late checkout at 4pm granted despite wedding event and fully booked hotel; at departure 8 women in white robes stood ready in courtyard with equipment—quite an impressive sight, showing the operational pressure of late checkout.

g155032-d185747.author: Lax
g155032-d185747.content: Room is not very spacious. Book two nights, get one free. After miscellaneous fees, actual charge for three nights was $737.54 USD, so not particularly good value. Hotel parking was full; had to park outside at around $30 CAD per day. Used the food credit at the hotel's only restaurant (Rosélys). Service was mediocre, food taste was average, but the ambiance was nice. No free WiFi at the hotel!

g60982-d114008.author: Nana0206
g60982-d114008.content: Budget room without balcony upgraded to budget room with balcony, with views of Diamond Head and a bit of ocean. FHR benefits: 1) One-level room upgrade 2) Complimentary buffet breakfast worth $50/person. Quite good - fresh made omelets, sashimi, various pastries, natto, soba noodles, beef, crepes, fruit 3) $100 credit usable for all hotel spending, activities, dining, shopping. Used for Saturday evening seafood buffet at $88/person. Ordered drinks separately; two people spent $230 total, final charge $130. Food was decent, prime rib and butter fish were excellent 4) Guaranteed 4pm late checkout, with turn-down service. Overall nice experience, but at $700/night without breakfast doesn't seem worth it; at $500/night with breakfast and $100 credit would be good, since average Oahu rate is $300+. At check-in and check-out, the front desk kept emphasizing we were only staying one night, making me feel like a second-class guest.

g4585027-d12616613.author: lolll
g4585027-d12616613.content: Breakfast was excellent, available whether booked through FHR or not. The hotel's infinity pool is absolutely stunning.

g34439-d7143309.author: DSZ
g34439-d7143309.content: Two reservations. First two nights both rooms received Deluxe One King Bed, City View. After discussion, second room's final night upgraded to One Bedroom Suite, Oceanfront. SNP initially did not post. Opened case with Marriott, attached FHR screenshot. SNP posted after 2 weeks.

g45963-d1829539.author: DSZ
g45963-d1829539.content: Always stays at Cosmo when in Vegas. Tried both FHR prepay and pay later. Never received SNP. Front desk says it recognizes Marriott member number, but never showed in app. Cannot open case with Marriott as there's no Marriott reservation number. Uncertain if booked through Cosmo's website for casino account.

g45963-d1829539.author: DSZ
g45963-d1829539.content: Never received SNP. Front desk recognizes Marriott member number but never showed in app.

g45963-d1829539.author: DSZ
g45963-d1829539.content: Never received SNP. Upgrades work fine without excessive negotiation, so no issues with Cosmo or Marriott.

g45963-d1829539.author: qgao8
g45963-d1829539.content: Hotel: The Cosmopolitan of Las Vegas. Stay: A Thursday in June 2022. Booking: FHR Prepay. Booked room: Two queen city view. Actual room: One king fountain view studio. Membership: Marriott Gold (from Platinum card). SNP/benefits: $100 spa credit—purchased two day passes at $60/person (Mon-Thu), excellent amenities with nice views of the pool across the way; can relax at both locations. $75 breakfast credit used at Wicked Spoon downstairs; Marriott Gold and above can use invited guest status for better pricing. Recommend arriving around 10:30am to use early-bird prices on the lunch menu, including snow crab legs (long line but generous portions). Early check-in basically guaranteed if not picky about room type. Check-in can include lounge access or water and coffee; rooms don't have water provided. Casino is not large but many elevators; nightclub scene is good with lots of attractive people. Bellagio or Crystal Mall very convenient for shopping. Hotel's interior design has a nightclub aesthetic. Fountain view allows seeing the fountains but not hearing the music; balcony furniture smells heavily of cigarette and marijuana smoke. Hygiene is average, service is excellent.

g45963-d23328374.author: qgao8
g45963-d23328374.content: Hotel: Crockfords Las Vegas. Stay: A Wednesday in June 2022. Booking: FHR prepay. Booked room: 1 King city view. Actual room: 1 King strip view. Membership: Hilton Gold (from Platinum card, automatically received room nights). SNP/benefits: $125 FHR credit usable almost everywhere—tried the street food restaurant twice, though Tiger Hall is not covered. Stir-fried rice cake and Hainanese chicken were good; skip the dumplings. $50 Hilton membership credit only works at DWAG bar—ordered fries and two drinks, decent. $33×2 breakfast credit—most buffets covered, though The Kitchen buffet was poor value; recommend ordering à la carte. Welcome amenity: 2 free drinks at bar/lounge after 4pm. Despite being an ordinary Wednesday, hotel was fully booked; three other FHR guests checking in at the same time received no upgrades and waited 1+ hour at the lobby to get room keys. Casino is not very large but brand new with no smoke smell. Dealers are good. Paid with Aspire card without issues; resort fees and miscellaneous charges were fully covered. Recommend visiting off-season for better room upgrade chances, though Vegas off-season is hard to predict.

g32655-d124956.author: huaweiping
g32655-d124956.content: Hotel: Hotel Figueroa. Stay: 5/28/2022 - 5/30/2022. Booking: Amex Travel THC. Booked room: Double bed (economy). Actual room: Double bed (economy); front desk indicated fully booked. Membership: Hyatt Gold. SNP/benefits: Yes, but didn't initially show in app. At check-in, informed front desk and showed membership card; benefits appeared after check-out. Rooms are not large, but with Amex's $200 credit, feels reasonable for DTLA. Booked two rooms, each received $100 credit, but unfortunately couldn't combine them.

g60814-d1159237.author: huaweiping
g60814-d1159237.content: Hotel: Andaz Savannah. Stay: 6/10/2022 - 6/12/2022. Booking: Amex Travel THC. Booked room: King bed (economy). Actual room: King bed (economy); front desk indicated fully booked. Membership: Hyatt Gold. SNP/benefits: Yes, showed directly in app. Savannah is a great vacation destination. The $100 credit used for in-hotel dining was decent, and it's thoughtful that gratuity is also covered by the credit. Recommend avoiding June-July—it's hot and humid. Locals recommend October or March through early April as best seasons.
